comments.author: @MorderElg
comments.text:
There was another symbol of resistance that I've never seen any video mention. Maybe because it was a word play that's hard to translate.
People carried a comb, partially visible. Like in a man's vest pocket. Not really that strange of a thing to carry. But the meaning was deeper. "Vi greier oss selv."  Translated to (both meanings): "We comb our own hair." "We can take it/handle it, ourselves."  We take care of ourselves and do fine without you, in spite of you. As it was nothing wrong with carrying a comb, and the true meaning , it was carried openly, though not drawing unnecessary attention to it. The Norwegians knew its meaning, while the nazis did not.

replies.author: @truehorizons66
replies.text: That is a fascinating piece of history, and I really appreciate you sharing such an insightful detail about the resistance.
